headlines.title: Nancy Pelosi, Donald Trump Bet On The Same 10 Stocks – Some Might Surprise You (CORRECTED)
headlines.summary: Editor’s Note: This story has been updated to correct details related to Intel (INTC). When it comes to tracking the trading activity of members of Congress, former Speaker of the House Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) has been among the most followed.

headlines.title: Forget AI Hyperscalers: Tesla May Own the Most Valuable AI Application
headlines.summary: While investors fixate on Tesla's vehicle deliveries and margins, a quieter transformation is underway that positions the company to compete directly with the hyperscalers reshaping the global economy.

headlines.title: Apple sues OpenAI alleging trade secret theft
headlines.summary: In a classic “Friday news dump,” Apple filed a federal lawsuit in California against OpenAI, accusing its once-partner of stealing some of its trade secrets. Specifically, it’s alleging that two of its former employees brought proprietary and confidential information about its hardware to OpenAI when they joined. The lawsuit calls to mind Waymo’s trade secret theft lawsuit against Uber, filed in 2017.

headlines.title: Palantir CEO: “Something Has Gone Completely Wrong” With OpenAI and Anthropic
headlines.summary: Alex Karp called out OpenAI and Anthropic by name on live television, sent Palantir stock surging 8%, and argued that enterprises are done paying for tokens. The question is whether his numbers back up the swagger or just paper over a valuation that has Michael Burry betting against it.

headlines.title: Uber and DoorDash Drivers Think They Have No 401(k). The IRS Lets Them Build a $72,000 One but Almost Nobody Does
headlines.summary: If you drive for Uber (NYSE:UBER), deliver for DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH), or shop for Instacart (NASDAQ:CART), you almost certainly get a 1099-NEC at year-end and no benefits package. That is exactly why the IRS treats you as both the employer and the employee of your own one-person business, and why a Solo 401(k) lets you shovel ... Uber and DoorDash Drivers Think They Have No 401(k).

description: Uber Technologies, Inc. develops and operates proprietary technology applications in the United States, Canada, Latin America, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and the Asia Pacific. The company operates through three segments: Mobility, Delivery, and Freight. The Mobility segment connects consumers with a range of transportation modalities, such as ridesharing, carsharing, micromobility, rentals, public transit, taxis, and other modalities; and offers riders in a variety of vehicle types, as well as financial partnerships products and advertising services. The Delivery segment allows consumers to search for and discover restaurants to grocery, alcohol, convenience, and other retailers, as well as order a meal or other items, and either pick-up at the restaurant or have it delivered; and provides Uber direct, a white-label delivery-as-a-service for retailers and restaurants, as well as advertising services. The Freight segment manages transportation and logistics networks, which connects shippers and carriers in digital marketplace, including carriers upfronts, pricing, and shipment booking; and offers on-demand platform to automate logistics end-to-end transactions for small-and medium-sized businesses to global enterprises. Uber Technologies, Inc. has staetegic partnership with Mews to embed ride booking, real-time tracking and integrated billing directly into the Mews platform. The company was formerly known as Ubercab, Inc. and changed its name to Uber Technologies, Inc. in February 2011. Uber Technologies, Inc. was founded in 2009 and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.
